creator: gkovacs@virusbuster.hu
text:
A multi-threaded (libthr; threads are detached) mail defense application
(AV) which uses some pthread functions (pthread_mutex_lock, pthread_create)
gets extremely slow beside huge (3<) load (around 50-100 thread). It works
correct when the load is less. 

The hardware is HP DL140, Dual Xeon 3.2. On a much slower machine with the
same os, kernel settings and almost the same mail traffic could not repeat
the problem.

According to ktrace and gprof the most time's spent in _umtx_op syscall:

ktrace output (kdump -E):
 71391 vbmsrv   0.000532 CALL  select(0x6,0xbf9fef00,0xbf9fee80,0xbf9fee00,0xbf9fddf8)
 71391 vbmsrv   0.000535 RET   _umtx_op 0

 71391 vbmsrv   0.000559 RET   _umtx_op 0
